question: Explain the schematic diagram of the secondary side equipment related to a single NPM (Nuclear Power Module).

nuscale_ch01_0116.title: Figure 1.7-3e: Piping and Instrumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0116.summary: This section provides a comprehensive list of legends and abbreviations used in NuScale’s Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the symbols for various analysis functions, measurement types, and instrumentation identifiers. It serves as a reference to interpret the technical drawings by defining acronyms for sensors, analyzers, and measurement methods related to process variables such as flow, radiation, chemical composition, and physical properties.

nuscale_ch01_0774.title: SRP 15.6.3, Rev 2: Radiological Consequences of Steam Generator Tube Failure (PWR)II (No Number) Last two sentences of the last paragraph of Section II on page 15.6.32 - Plant-Specific Technical Specifications for Primary and Secondary Coolant System Iodine Activity
nuscale_ch01_0774.summary: The section explains that while the guidance regarding technical specifications for primary and secondary coolant iodine activity applies to the NuScale Design Certification Application (DCA), the radiological acceptance criteria specified in SRP 15.6.3 are superseded by those in SRP Section 15.0.3. Therefore, the NuScale application partially conforms to the guidance, following the updated acceptance criteria in SRP 15.0.3 instead.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information. It references tables and figures that list and explain the symbols and diagrams used, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0036.title: Refueling Operations
nuscale_ch01_0036.summary: Refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM) are conducted independently from other modules. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling pool, disassembling it for inspection, maintenance, and refueling, and then reassembling and returning it to service after successful testing and startup procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0510.title: DSRS 11.5, Rev 0: Process and Effluent Radiological Monitoring Instrumentation and Sampling SystemsII.7 Descriptions of design features and instrumentation used in primary and secondary coolant system leakage detection
nuscale_ch01_0510.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological monitoring and sampling systems, specifically regarding design features and instrumentation for detecting leakage in primary and secondary coolant systems. It notes that most criteria are met, with some site-specific operational program requirements deferred to the COL applicant. Partial conformance is indicated for compliance with certain post-TMI action plan items, as some aspects are the responsibility of the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0018.title: Principal Design Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0018.summary: The section describes the principal design criteria for the NuScale plant, focusing on the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS) and the overall facility process. It outlines the reactor core's heat transfer process, natural circulation of coolant, steam generation, and the flow of superheated steam to the turbine and condenser, emphasizing the system's role in efficient heat removal and power generation.

nuscale_ch01_0058.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Electrically Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0058.summary: This section describes the critical heat flux (CHF) testing conducted for the NuScale NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, which uses AREVA HMP™/HTP™ spacer grid technology. The tests, performed at the AREVA Karlstein Thermal Hydraulics facility, aimed to gather CHF data, validate and develop CHF correlations, and collect thermal-hydraulic performance data under various operating conditions using an electrically heated 5x5 simulated fuel bundle.

nuscale_ch01_0035.title: Fuel Handling and Reactor Maintenance Areas
nuscale_ch01_0035.summary: The fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas are located at the west end of the Reactor Building and include spaces for the spent fuel pool, refueling pool, and dry dock, with facilities designed for safe fuel handling and maintenance operations. These areas provide radiation protection for personnel and include equipment and access for receiving, inspecting, storing, and transporting both new and spent fuel assemblies.

nuscale_ch01_0710.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.F Performance of Nonsafety-Related Systems and Single Failures of Active and Passive Systems
nuscale_ch01_0710.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets the regulatory criteria outlined in DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5 for events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed). It notes that the NuScale design does not include a steam pressure regulator and that all relevant safety and performance criteria, including fuel cladding integrity, incident frequency, instrument setpoints, and single failure considerations, are met.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, such as fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management, aimed at minimizing human error and meeting regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0711.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.3 Analytical Model
nuscale_ch01_0711.summary: This section confirms that the analytical models and parameter values (including initial power level, scram characteristics, core burnup, and instrumentation setpoints) used for evaluating events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed) conform to the requirements of DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5. No exceptions or deviations from the regulatory criteria are noted.

nuscale_ch01_0451.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsPSGD B.1 thru B.4Preheat Steam Generator Designs
nuscale_ch01_0451.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use top-feed, preheat, or once-through steam generator designs, so the specific regulatory guidelines for avoiding water hammers in these systems are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a different system (DHRS) that operates via passive natural circulation and inherently avoids the water hammer issues addressed by these guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0452.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsOTSGD B.1 Once-Through Steam Generator Designs - Auxiliary Feedwater Supply
nuscale_ch01_0452.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use the steam generator configurations (top-feed, preheat, or once-through with auxiliary feedwater) addressed by BTP 10-2, Rev 4, and therefore the associated water hammer guidelines are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a passive Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) that fulfills a similar safety function without the need for auxiliary feedwater pumps or external supply headers, inherently avoiding the water hammer issues these guidelines target.

nuscale_ch01_0029.title: Power Conversion System
nuscale_ch01_0029.summary: The Power Conversion System in the NuScale plant includes a main steam system, turbine generator, condenser, cooling tower, and condensate/feedwater system, allowing individual NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) to be operated or maintained independently. The plant also features various cooling water systems—such as the reactor component cooling water system, pool cooling systems, and a circulating water system—to support efficient heat removal and plant operation.

edges.description: Section A provides the legend for instrumentation and analysis function identifiers used in schematic diagrams, which are necessary to interpret the secondary side equipment schematics referenced in Section B's discussion of plant-specific technical specifications for secondary coolant system iodine activity.
Section A provides the legend and symbol definitions necessary to interpret the schematic diagrams of secondary side equipment presented in Section B.

Section A provides the legend and symbol definitions necessary to interpret schematic diagrams, which are essential for understanding the secondary side equipment layout referenced in Section B's description of refueling operations for a single NPM.

Section A provides the legend for instrumentation symbols used in schematic diagrams, which are necessary to interpret the design features and instrumentation described in Section B for secondary coolant system leakage detection.

Section A provides the legend and identifiers necessary to interpret schematic diagrams, such as those describing the secondary side equipment referenced in Section B's overview of plant systems and equipment.

Section A provides the legend for instrumentation symbols used in schematic diagrams, which is necessary to interpret the equipment and measurement devices shown in the secondary side schematic referenced in the analysis question, while Section B discusses thermal-hydraulic performance testing data that would be represented and interpreted using such schematic diagrams and their legends.

Section A provides the legend for instrumentation symbols used in schematic diagrams, which is necessary to interpret the piping and instrumentation diagrams referenced in Section B's description of secondary side equipment and connections for a single NPM.

Section A references regulatory requirements for secondary coolant system iodine activity, while Section B provides schematic diagrams (P&IDs) that visually clarify the design and configuration of the secondary side equipment, thus supporting the demonstration of compliance with those requirements.

Section A discusses regulatory requirements for secondary coolant system iodine activity, which are relevant to the schematic and operational boundaries of secondary side equipment described in Section B during refueling operations for a single NPM.

Section A discusses regulatory requirements for monitoring iodine activity in the primary and secondary coolant systems, while Section B describes the design features and instrumentation used for detecting leakage in these systems, thus Section B provides specific technical details that support compliance with the requirements outlined in Section A.

Section A discusses regulatory requirements for secondary coolant system iodine activity in the context of steam generator tube failure, while Section B provides a general plant description including the secondary side equipment (such as the Site Cooling Water System) relevant to the schematic diagram requested in the analysis question, thus Section B provides the design context that Section A's regulatory requirements apply to.

Section A discusses regulatory requirements for iodine activity in the secondary coolant system, while Section B describes thermal-hydraulic performance testing of the steam generator, which provides operational data relevant to ensuring the secondary side meets those regulatory limits.

Section A discusses regulatory requirements for monitoring iodine activity in the secondary coolant system, which is directly related to the schematic and operational details of secondary side equipment described in Section B, as these systems must be designed and maintained to meet such radiological safety specifications.

Section A provides detailed schematic diagrams and drawings that visually clarify the design and arrangement of secondary side equipment, which are referenced or implicitly relied upon in Section B's description of refueling operations for a single NPM, as understanding the equipment layout is necessary for explaining the operational steps.

Section A provides detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams that visually clarify the design and layout of secondary side equipment, which supports Section B's regulatory requirement to describe design features and instrumentation used for leakage detection in the secondary coolant system.

Section A provides detailed schematic diagrams and drawings that visually clarify the design and operation of secondary side equipment, which supports and specifies the general plant and system descriptions outlined in Section B.

Section A provides schematic diagrams and drawings that visually represent the secondary side equipment of a single NPM, while Section B details the thermal-hydraulic performance testing and data that inform the technical requirements and validation of the equipment depicted in those diagrams.

Section A provides detailed schematic diagrams and drawings of secondary side equipment, which visually clarify the design and layout of components whose locations and classifications are described in Section B for the fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas.

Section A describes the operational process of moving an NPM for refueling, while Section B outlines the design features and instrumentation for detecting leakage in the secondary coolant system, both of which are relevant to understanding the schematic diagram of secondary side equipment as they provide context on equipment function and monitoring requirements.

Section A describes the operational process of moving an NPM for refueling, while Section B outlines the design and function of the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS), which is part of the secondary side equipment relevant to the schematic diagram requested in the analysis question.

Section A describes the process of moving an NPM for refueling, which relies on the manual connection and disconnection of pipe fittings and electrical connections described in Section B as part of the fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas.

Section A describes the instrumentation and design features for detecting leakage in the secondary coolant system, while Section B provides a general description of the secondary side equipment (such as the Site Cooling Water System) and its role in plant operation, thus Section A specifies the monitoring and detection aspects for the systems generally described in Section B.

Section A describes the instrumentation and design features for detecting leakage in the secondary coolant system, while Section B provides test data and validation for the thermal-hydraulic performance of the steam generator, which is a key component of the secondary side equipment referenced in Section A.

Section A describes the instrumentation and design features for detecting leakage in the secondary coolant system, while Section B details the physical layout and connections of secondary side equipment (such as pipe fittings and electrical connections) relevant to a single NPM, thus Section B provides specific equipment context that Section A's monitoring systems are intended to oversee.

Section A provides a general description of the plant systems, including the secondary side equipment (such as the Site Cooling Water System), while Section B details testing and validation of steam generator performance, which supports the adequacy and design of the secondary side equipment described in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant's cooling systems and reactor core location, while Section B details the physical arrangements and connections for handling and maintaining the NPM, both contributing specific information necessary to explain the schematic diagram of secondary side equipment related to a single NPM.

Section A discusses thermal-hydraulic performance and critical heat flux testing for the NuScale fuel design, which underpins the safe operation of the NPM, while Section B describes the physical layout and connections for handling and maintaining the NPM, both of which are necessary to understand the schematic diagram of secondary side equipment as they address different but complementary aspects (performance data and physical configuration).

Section A discusses regulatory requirements and system performance for secondary side events in a NuScale NPM, while Section B provides the diagram legends necessary to interpret the piping and instrumentation diagrams referenced in those analyses, thus enabling understanding of the equipment layout described in Section A.

Section A discusses the performance and conformance of secondary side systems (such as turbine trip and main steam isolation) in the context of accident scenarios, while Section B addresses the technical specifications for secondary coolant system iodine activity, both of which are connected through their focus on the secondary side equipment's role in safety and regulatory compliance for a single NPM.
Section B provides the schematic diagrams and detailed drawings referenced in Section A to visually clarify the secondary side equipment discussed in the regulatory analysis.

Section A describes the Central Utility Building and lists secondary side equipment relevant to a single NPM, while Section B provides the diagram legends necessary to interpret schematic diagrams of such equipment, thus Section B specifies how to read the schematic representations of the equipment described in Section A.

Section A describes the secondary side equipment (e.g., chiller, chemical treatment) housed in the Central Utility Building, which are relevant to the schematic diagram requested in the analysis question, while Section B references regulatory requirements for secondary coolant system iodine activity that the described equipment must help satisfy.

Section A lists secondary side equipment (e.g., chiller, chemical treatment) located in the Central Utility Building, while Section B provides schematic diagrams (P&IDs) that visually represent such equipment and their interconnections, thus detailing the layout and function of the secondary side equipment referenced in Section A.

Section A discusses the analytical modeling and regulatory conformance of secondary side equipment scenarios, while Section B provides the diagram legends necessary to interpret the piping and instrumentation diagrams referenced in such analyses, thus enabling understanding of the schematic representations used in Section A.

Section A discusses analytical models and parameter values for secondary side transients in a single NPM, while Section B addresses technical specifications for secondary coolant system iodine activity, both of which are connected through their focus on the secondary side equipment and its operational and safety requirements.

Section A references the analytical models and parameter values used to evaluate secondary side transients, while Section B provides the piping and instrumentation diagrams that visually clarify the layout and components of the secondary side equipment discussed in Section A.

Section A discusses the performance and regulatory conformance of nonsafety-related systems during specific secondary side events, while Section B describes the Central Utility Building that houses nonsafety-related secondary side equipment such as chiller and demineralized water systems, thus Section B provides specific examples of the types of equipment referenced in Section A.

Section A discusses the performance and conformance of secondary side equipment and system responses to specific transients, while Section B provides the analytical models and parameter values used to evaluate those same transients, thus Section B specifies the technical basis for the assessments described in Section A.

Section A describes the Central Utility Building and its housed equipment, some of which (e.g., chiller equipment, chemical treatment for demineralized water) are part of the secondary side systems whose schematic diagram is analyzed in Section B's context of transient scenarios (e.g., turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum) affecting a single NPM.

Section A discusses design guidelines and regulatory criteria for secondary side equipment, while Section B provides the diagram legends necessary to interpret schematic diagrams of such equipment, thus Section B specifies the symbols and identifiers referenced or required for understanding the schematic context described in Section A.

Section A describes design guidelines and system functions for secondary side equipment (such as the DHRS in NuScale) while Section B addresses regulatory requirements for radiological consequences and technical specifications (e.g., iodine activity) that the secondary side equipment must help satisfy, connecting them through the need for the equipment's design to meet these safety and radiological criteria.

Section B provides the schematic diagrams and drawings that visually clarify the secondary side equipment of a single NPM, which are referenced and explained in Section A's discussion of design guidelines and regulatory conformance.

Section A discusses the design guidelines and regulatory context for secondary side equipment (such as the DHRS) in NuScale NPMs, while Section B provides the legend for interpreting the schematic diagrams (P&IDs) of such equipment, thus Section B enables understanding of the diagrams referenced or implied in Section A.

Section A describes design guidelines and regulatory conformance for secondary side equipment (specifically auxiliary feedwater systems) in the context of NuScale's DHRS, while Section B addresses the regulatory requirements for radiological consequences and technical specifications (including iodine activity) for the secondary coolant system, thus both sections are connected through their focus on regulatory compliance and design considerations for secondary side equipment in a single NPM.

Section B provides the detailed schematic diagrams and drawings that visually clarify the secondary side equipment referenced in Section A's discussion of design guidelines and regulatory conformance for the NuScale NPM.

Section A and Section B both reference BTP 10-2, Rev 4 and discuss its applicability to the NuScale DHRS as analogous to the AFW system in large PWRs, specifically in the context of design guidelines for avoiding water hammers in steam generators.

Section A describes the secondary side equipment of the NPM in general terms and references a figure (Figure 1.2-3), while Section B provides the legend for interpreting symbols used in piping and instrumentation diagrams, which would be necessary to understand any schematic diagram referenced in Section A.

Section A describes the components and configuration of the secondary side equipment (e.g., main steam system, condenser, feedwater system) for a single NPM, which are the systems whose radiological performance and iodine activity are regulated as discussed in Section B.

Section A provides a general description of the secondary side equipment in the power conversion system, while Section B specifies that detailed schematic diagrams (such as P&IDs) are provided to visually clarify the design described in Section A.
